However, an official DG circular (issued 6 February 2026) granted Ghanaian ordinary, official/service, and diplomatic passport holders visa-free access with immediate effect following the MOU signed 5 February 2026. The official immigration website has not yet been updated to reflect this change as of validation date (June 2026). Classification follows the official website listing; see gaps for the pending update.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities ordinarily requiring a visa to enter Zambia
e_visa.notes: All persons who ordinarily require visas to come to Zambia are eligible to apply for e-visas via eservices.zambiaimmigration.gov.zm. This includes both VOA-eligible nationalities (who may choose to apply in advance) and the 51 nationalities requiring visa prior to travel (who must apply online before arrival and cannot get VOA). Single entry USD 25, Double entry USD 40, Multiple entry USD 75. Visa valid 90 days from date of first issuance. Persons below age 16 cannot apply for an e-Visa independently and must be included in the travelling adult's application.

visa_policy.notes: As of 1 January 2025 (Statutory Instrument No. 78 of 2024), Zambia waived visa requirements for nationals of 53 additional countries, bringing the total visa-free count to 167 on the official list. The official page at zambiaimmigration.gov.zm/nationals-who-dont-require-visa/ lists 167 entries including overseas territories and dependencies. Nationals requiring visa prior to travel (51 listed nationalities) must apply online via the e-Services portal before travelling and cannot obtain VOA. KAZA UNIVISA (USD 50, valid 30 days, covers Zambia and Zimbabwe with day-trips to Botswana via Kazungula) is available at KKIA, HMNIA, Victoria Falls border, and Kazungula border. Zambia-Ghana bilateral MOU (signed 5 Feb 2026, effective 6 Feb 2026) grants ordinary, official/service, and diplomatic Ghanaian passport holders visa-free access; however, the official immigration website had not yet updated its nationality lists to reflect this as of June 2026 validation date.

conditional_access.conditions: A Gratis Visa (free of charge) is issued at Zambia Missions Abroad or at ports of entry to members of the Diplomatic Community on presentation of letters of accreditation and/or Diplomatic Passports. Also issued to diplomats not accredited to Zambia but visiting on official business on presentation of letters of invitation. Applies to diplomatic passport holders of any nationality. No fee charged.

Holders of Indian diplomatic passports are exempt from visa requirements under the Memorandum of Understanding on Exemption of Visa Requirements for Holders of Diplomatic Passports between the Government of the Republic of India and the Government of the Republic of Zambia. Signed 11 April 2018; entered into force 14 January 2019.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on official Zambia government sources. The Citizenship of Zambia Act No. 33 of 2016 permits dual citizenship, but there is no dedicated CBI program. Naturalisation requires lawful residence.

programs.name: Investors Permit
programs.notes: Issued to a foreigner (above 18 years) establishing or investing in a business in Zambia. Requires proof of personal investment of USD 250,000 for a new business or USD 150,000 if joining an existing company. Requires Certificate of Incorporation, Certificate of Minimum Capital/Share Capital, Investment License from ZDA (if available), and proof of property ownership or lease. After holding an Investors Permit for more than 3 years, the holder may qualify for a Residence Permit (permanent residence).

programs.name: Residence Permit (Permanent Residence)
programs.notes: Confers permanent residence. Eligible categories include: holders of an Employment Permit for a continuous period exceeding 10 years; holder of Investor's Permit for more than 3 years; holder of Spouse Permit for at least 5 years; persons born from a citizen; retirees showing proof of sufficient funds (pension, irrevocable annuity, retirement account). A residence permit ceases to be valid if the holder is absent from Zambia for any period in excess of twelve months.

programs.name: Employment Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals employed in Zambia by government, private sector, NGOs, or religious organizations for a period exceeding 6 months. Requires employer (or practicing lawyer/immigration consultant) to apply on behalf of the expatriate. Applicant must remain outside Zambia until permit is approved.
programs.notes: Valid up to 10 years (extendable). Holder must observe conditions on permitted area and nature of employment. Holder of an Employment Permit cannot buy land. After 10 consecutive years, holder may apply for Residence Permit (permanent). Requires local newspaper advertisements in two leading newspapers and a succession/training plan for knowledge transfer.

programs.name: Temporary Employment Permit
programs.eligibility: Business visitors intending to remain in Zambia for more than 30 days. Validity shall not exceed 6 months within any 12-month period.
programs.notes: Requires covering letter from the institution doing business with applicant and completed application form (Form 25).

agreements: SADC Protocol on the Facilitation of Movement of Persons (Zambia has ratified)
Zambia-Ghana Mutual Visa Waiver MOU (signed 5 February 2026, effective 6 February 2026; covers ordinary, official/service, and diplomatic passport holders of both nations)

validation_notes: Re-validated against live official sources on 27 June 2026 and again on 29 June 2026.
Visa-free list (167 entries) confirmed unchanged on zambiaimmigration.gov.zm/nationals-who-dont-require-visa/.

Visa-on-arrival list (34 entries) confirmed as matching the prior record with one significant update: Ghana is still listed as VOA on the official website, but a DG circular (6 Feb 2026) granted Ghanaian holders of ordinary, official/service, and diplomatic biometric passports visa-free access immediately following the MOU of 5 Feb 2026. The official website has not yet been updated. Ghana's VOA entry now includes a note explaining this discrepancy.
Agreements section updated to include the Zambia-Ghana Mutual Visa Waiver MOU (Feb 2026).

For-Visitors page confirmed: Single entry USD 25, Double entry USD 40, Multiple entry USD 75, Transit USD 25, Day Tripper USD 10, KAZA UNIVISA USD 50. Visa valid 90 days from date of first issuance. Standard visitors receive 90 days in any 12-month period.
KAZA UNIVISA details confirmed: USD 50, valid 30 days, covers Zambia and Zimbabwe with day-trips to Botswana via Kazungula. Available at KKIA, HMNIA, Victoria Falls border, Kazungula border.
Permit Types page confirmed: Investors Permit minimum USD 250,000 (new business) or USD 150,000 (joining existing company). No changes to fast_track or rbi data required.
e_visa notes updated to clarify that VOA-eligible nationalities may also apply in advance via the portal.
Swaziland notes updated to flag that the country is now officially named Eswatini (the official page still lists 'Swaziland').
Serbia and Montenegro notes updated to flag that this entity no longer exists; official page has not updated this listing.
No conditional access rules based on holding third-country visas or residence permits were found on any official source.
gaps: The official visa-free list page does not specify exact max stay days per nationality; the 90-day figure is taken from the For Visitors page ('All ordinary Visitors...are entitled to a free ninety (90) days visit in any period of twelve (12) months').
The visa-on-arrival page does not specify max stay days for that category; days granted depend on the type of visa issued at arrival.
The official page still lists 'Serbia and Montenegro' as a single entity (defunct since 2006); Zambia's website has not split this into Serbia (SRB) and Montenegro (MNE).
The official page still lists 'Swaziland' rather than the current official name 'Eswatini' (SWZ).

Ghana-Zambia visa waiver MOU (5 Feb 2026, effective 6 Feb 2026) was confirmed via an official DG circular, but the immigration website had not been updated to move Ghana from VOA to visa-free as of 13 June 2026. No statutory instrument number confirmed for this change.

Max stay days for the Ghana-Zambia waiver not confirmed in official sources; secondary sources cite 30-day limit per trip for Ghana's Cabinet-approved waiver (April 2026) but this is not confirmed from a Zambia government source.
No golden visa, digital nomad visa, or other special residence programs found on official Zambia immigration sources.
COMESA: Zambia is a COMESA member but has not signed/ratified the COMESA Protocol on Free Movement of Persons.
Dual citizenship allowed per Citizenship of Zambia Act No. 33 of 2016 - confirmed from secondary government sources but not from main immigration website.
No conditional entry rules (e.g., based on holding a valid US/UK/Schengen visa or residence permit) found on any official Zambia immigration source.
No OCI or other special diaspora status rules for Zambia found on any official source.
The India-Zambia MOU (diplomatic passports only, signed 11 April 2018) was sourced from India MEA; the underlying Zambia-side document was not independently confirmed.

The Gratis Visa policy for diplomatic community members was sourced from a 2019 Zambia Immigration Guide PDF; the main immigration website does not publish an updated list of bilateral diplomatic passport waiver agreements.

Whether 'official passport' holders (in addition to diplomatic passport holders) also receive the Gratis Visa was not confirmed from the official Zambia Immigration Guide; the 2019 PDF only mentions 'Diplomatic Passports'.

Puerto Rico and US Minor Outlying Islands appear separately on the official VOA list despite not issuing their own passports (residents travel on US passports, which are visa-free). These appear to be data artifacts on the official website.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national one entry into Zambia for tourism or short-term visits.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-visa portal (eservices.zambiaimmigration.gov.zm) and on arrival at ports of entry. Persons under 16 cannot apply for an e-Visa independently and must be included in the travelling adult's application. Requires passport with at least 6 months validity and 3 blank pages.

visa_types.name: Double Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Permits two entries into Zambia during the validity period for tourism or short-term visits.
visa_types.notes: Allows two entries during the 90-day validity period. Available via e-visa portal and on arrival.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows unlimited entries into Zambia during the validity period for tourism or short-term visits.
visa_types.notes: Issued by Headquarters through the e-visa facility. Unlimited entries during the 90-day validity period.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals passing through Zambia overland who require a visa.
visa_types.notes: Maximum stay of 7 days. Intended for overland transit passengers who are visa-required nationals.

visa_types.name: Day Tripper Visa
visa_types.purpose: For short day visits of under 24 hours at the same port of entry, typically for Victoria Falls visitors.
visa_types.notes: Available only at Victoria Falls and Kazungula Border Controls. Currently only issued offline (not available via e-visa portal). For visits under 24 hours returning to same port.

visa_types.name: KAZA UNIVISA
visa_types.purpose: A regional visa covering Zambia and Zimbabwe, with permitted day trips to Botswana via Kazungula, for tourism in the Kavango-Zambezi Transfrontier Conservation Area.
visa_types.notes: Covers Zambia and Zimbabwe with multiple entries between the two countries. Includes day trips to Botswana via Kazungula border. Available at Kenneth Kaunda International Airport (KKIA), Harry Mwanga Nkumbula International Airport (HMNIA), Victoria Falls border, and Kazungula border. Valid up to 30 days within the regional area.

visa_types.name: Gratis Visa
visa_types.purpose: A complimentary visa issued free of charge to members of the diplomatic community entering Zambia on official or accredited business.
visa_types.notes: Issued at Zambia Missions Abroad or at ports of entry to members of the Diplomatic Community presenting letters of accreditation and/or diplomatic passports. Also issued to diplomats not accredited to Zambia but visiting on official business with letters of invitation. No fee charged.

visa_types.name: Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Zambia for employment purposes for a period exceeding 6 months.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 10 years (extendable). Covers government employees, private sector workers, NGO staff, volunteers, religious organization employees, and spouses or adult children working in family businesses. Requires employment contract, police clearance, CV, qualifications, and a succession/training plan. Holder cannot purchase land or engage in studies. Issuance fee: 24,240 ZMW; Renewal: 28,280 ZMW. After 10 consecutive years, holder may apply for Residence Permit (permanent residence).

visa_types.name: Temporary Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For business visitors intending to remain in Zambia for more than 30 days but not exceeding 6 months.
visa_types.notes: Maximum validity of 6 months per 12-month period. Requires covering letter from the institution the applicant is doing business with, completed Form 25, passport, and photograph.

visa_types.name: Investor's Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals aged 18 and above who wish to establish a new business or invest in an existing company in Zambia.
visa_types.notes: Requires proof of personal investment of USD 250,000 for a new business or USD 150,000 for joining an existing company. Requires Certificate of Incorporation, proof of investment, lease or ownership agreement, bank statements, and ZRA tax clearance for renewals. Issuance fee: 16,160 ZMW; Renewal: 20,200 ZMW. After holding an Investor's Permit for more than 3 years, holder may qualify for a Residence Permit (permanent residence).

visa_types.name: Study Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals undertaking full-time or part-time studies at accredited Zambian educational institutions for longer than 3 months.
visa_types.notes: Renewable for the duration of studies. Requires letter of acceptance from institution, medical report from a Zambian government facility, sponsor letter, and proof of sponsor's financial status. Holders cannot engage in paid work. Issuance fee: 9,090 ZMW; Renewal: 12,120 ZMW.

visa_types.name: Spouse Permit
visa_types.purpose: For spouses of Zambian citizens, established residents, or Residence Permit holders to reside in Zambia.
visa_types.notes: Renewable. Requires marriage certificate, spouse's consent letter, proof of spouse's status, and proof of sustenance. After holding a Spouse Permit for at least 5 years, holder may apply for a Residence Permit (permanent residence).

visa_types.name: Residence Permit (Retiree)
visa_types.purpose: Grants permanent residence to qualifying retirees who can demonstrate sufficient funds to support themselves in Zambia.
visa_types.notes: Retirees qualify for Residence Permit by demonstrating proof of sufficient funds (pension, irrevocable annuity, or retirement account). This is one of several eligibility routes for the Residence Permit. Permit ceases to be valid if holder is absent from Zambia for any period exceeding 12 months. Issuance fee: 20,200 ZMW.

visa_types.name: Visiting Permit
visa_types.purpose: For visitors who wish to remain in Zambia beyond the standard 90-day visa-free or visa entitlement period.
visa_types.notes: Issued for 3 months; maximum 9 months within any 12-month period. Requires host consent letter, host's proof of immigration status, return ticket or proof of sufficient funds, and valid passport.

visa_types.name: Cross Border Permit
visa_types.purpose: For members of regional trade groupings and cross-border traders from neighboring countries engaging in trade across Zambia's borders.
visa_types.notes: Issued for 90 days, renewable for a further 90 days. Maximum 6 months per 12-month period. Requires proof of membership in a cross-border traders' association and a support letter from that association.

visa_types.name: Transit Permit
visa_types.purpose: For commercial truck drivers and crew members transiting through Zambia.
visa_types.notes: Issued for 1 year, renewable for a further 1 year. Distinct from the Transit Visa (which is for overland transit by air/land passengers). Intended for commercial transport operators and crew. Requires company or host letter, passport, and photograph.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Permit
visa_types.purpose: For ambassadors, diplomats, consular officers, international organization representatives, and their immediate families residing in Zambia.
visa_types.notes: Requires Ministry of Foreign Affairs support letter, Note Verbale or accreditation letter, diplomatic passport, and diplomatic identity card. Covers immediate family members of the permit holder.

visa_types.name: Asylum Seeker's Permit
visa_types.purpose: Issued to individuals who have entered Zambia and are seeking asylum, allowing them to remain lawfully during refugee status determination.
visa_types.notes: Issued for 30 days for the purpose of refugee status determination. Requires national registration card or passport if available. No fee charged.

outbound_official.note: no official Zambia government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data
